title: Comparettia langlassei
taxonomy: Comparettia langlassei is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of epiphytic orchid, which means it grows on other plants or objects for support. Comparettia langlassei is native to the tropical regions of South America. This species was first described by the American botanist Robert Louis Dressler in 1993. Comparettia langlassei is classified in the genus Comparettia, which is part of the subtribe Oncidiinae.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as provide relief from digestive problems and skin conditions.
